top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Diplomacy over two wars led the day: European leaders Macron, Starmer and Merz will meet Zelensky in London on June 7 after Zelensky's open letter to Putin seeking a ceasefire, while the US drafted a resolution condemning Iran at the IAEA as Iran reaffirmed support for Hezbollah and a wider peace deal looked in doubt. In the US, the Senate approved US$70 billion for Trump's immigration crackdown and Washington pressed tariffs over forced labour, a claim Norway rejected. Off-Earth, NASA put ISS astronauts on evacuation alert over a worsening air leak.

stories.headline: NASA puts ISS astronauts on evacuation alert after worsening air leak
stories.summary: NASA placed International Space Station crew on evacuation alert after an air leak worsened, weighing options as engineers assess the risk.

stories.headline: European leaders to meet Zelensky in London on June 7 after his open letter to Putin
stories.summary: France's Macron, the UK's Starmer and Germany's Merz will meet Zelensky in London on June 7, the Elysee said, after Zelensky issued an open letter to Putin calling for a meeting and ceasefire. The Kremlin said Putin had been briefed on the letter; no Russian commitment was reported.

stories.headline: US drafts IAEA resolution condemning Iran as Tehran backs Hezbollah
stories.summary: The US is preparing a draft resolution condemning Iran at the IAEA, diplomats say, while Iran declared support for Hezbollah and prospects for a wider peace deal appeared in doubt. Details remain at the diplomatic-claim stage.

stories.headline: US Senate approves US$70 billion for Trump immigration crackdown
stories.summary: The US Senate approved US$70 billion in funding for the Trump administration's immigration enforcement crackdown.

stories.headline: Trump presses tariffs over forced labour; Norway rejects the claim
stories.summary: The Trump administration floated fresh tariffs framed around forced labour in supply chains; analysts said tariffs are no fix for the global issue, and Norway rejected the US claim and opposed the tariffs.

stories.headline: WHO announces six-month plan to fight Ebola
stories.summary: The WHO announced a six-month plan to combat the ongoing Ebola outbreak. The reported funding figure varied within the source (headline US$668m vs slug US$518m), so the exact amount is left unconfirmed pending WHO's primary statement.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A busy local news day dominated by crime and the courts: another Singaporean was arrested over a Cambodia-based scam syndicate, a mother was jailed four years for letting her 15-year-old son smoke meth, and ICA caught 30 motorists queue-cutting at Woodlands Checkpoint over the Vesak long weekend. On the lighter side, Singapore Airlines is reportedly in talks for a major new jet order, and 58 venues islandwide will screen World Cup 2026 matches live for free.

stories.headline: Singapore Airlines in talks for major new jet order
stories.summary: SIA is reportedly in talks with Airbus and Boeing for a major widebody order, sources say, as the carrier plans fleet growth. Figures (around 50 jets) are unconfirmed by the airline.

stories.headline: Another Singaporean arrested over Cambodia-based scam syndicate
stories.summary: Police arrested a Singaporean for alleged links to a Cambodia-based scam ring; reports say around 30 men remain at large. Part of a wider crackdown on transnational scam operations.

stories.headline: 30 motorists caught queue-cutting at Woodlands Checkpoint over long weekend
stories.summary: ICA caught 30 motorists cutting queue and committing other traffic offences at Woodlands Checkpoint over the Vesak Day long weekend, warning of enforcement action.

stories.headline: Mother jailed 4 years for letting 15-year-old son smoke meth
stories.summary: A mother was sentenced to four years' jail for allowing her 15-year-old son to consume methamphetamine and leaving drug utensils within his reach.

stories.headline: Singapore to ease restrictions on new hotels and hostels in heritage areas
stories.summary: Authorities will relax rules on operating new hotels and hostels in heritage precincts such as Upper Circular Road and Beach Road, part of efforts to rejuvenate the areas.

stories.headline: 58 venues islandwide to screen World Cup 2026 matches live for free
stories.summary: Selected World Cup 2026 matches will be screened live at 58 community venues — including community clubs, sports centres and The Kallang — for free public viewing.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All four theatres are escalating. Russia and Ukraine traded heavy long-range blows: a June 1–2 Russian missile-drone barrage killed at least 22 in Ukraine (Ukraine's Air Force reported 73 missiles and 656 drones launched, most intercepted), and on June 3 Ukrainian drones struck a St. Petersburg oil terminal and, Kyiv says, a Baltic Fleet corvette at Kronstadt. In the Gulf, CENTCOM said a US jet disabled an Iran-bound tanker on June 2 and that Iranian missile/drone attacks on US forces on June 3 all failed or were intercepted — Iran's claims are disputed. A US-brokered Israel–Lebanon ceasefire announced June 3 was rejected by Hezbollah, and Israeli strikes and evacuation orders across south Lebanon continued into June 5. Gaza's October 2025 truce holds only nominally, with Israeli strikes still reported killing roughly 9–11 Palestinians a day.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Both sides escalating long-range strikes; Ukraine hits St. Petersburg, Russia's Jun 1–2 barrage kills at least 22

timeline.event: Russia launched a large overnight barrage on Ukraine, killing at least 22 people and toppling an apartment building, officials said. Ukraine's Air Force said Russia fired 73 missiles and 656 drones, of which it destroyed or suppressed 40 missiles and 602 drones.
Officials reported the same overnight Russian missile-and-drone assault killed at least 22 and wounded dozens, corroborating the Ukrainian Air Force's tally of 73 missiles and 656 drones launched.

Ukrainian long-range drones struck and set ablaze a St. Petersburg oil terminal — over 1,000 km from the front — hours before Russia opened its St. Petersburg economic forum. Zelensky said Ukraine targeted only war-related sites and would escalate long-range strikes.

Ukraine's military said the same night's drones also hit the Russian Baltic Fleet guided-missile corvette Boikiy in dry dock at Kronstadt; Russia said its air defences downed more than 350 Ukrainian drones over several regions. Damage to the corvette is unconfirmed beyond Ukrainian-released video.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Blockade brinkmanship intensifies; US disables Iran-bound tanker, Iran fires missiles at US forces (CENTCOM says all failed)

timeline.event: After roughly five weeks of fighting, the US and Iran agreed a ceasefire that also covered Israel; tensions then shifted to a standoff over access to the Strait of Hormuz, with a US naval blockade of Iranian ports begun April 13.
Iran's state media said Tehran suspended nuclear/ceasefire talks with Washington over Israeli operations in Lebanon and Gaza; the US said diplomacy remained on track.

US Central Command said a US fighter jet fired a Hellfire missile into the engine room of the Botswana-flagged tanker M/T Lexie, disabling it before it reached Iran's Kharg Island after it allegedly ignored blockade warnings. CENTCOM said US forces have disabled six vessels and redirected 122 since April 13.

Iran's IRGC said it struck the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and a US base with missiles and drones, in response to a US strike on a Qeshm Island IRGC site. CENTCOM said all Iranian attacks failed or were intercepted and no US personnel were harmed — the Iranian claims are disputed and not independently confirmed.

Live coverage reported Iran firing missiles and drones as 'warning shots' at US warships in the Gulf of Oman, accusing Washington of harassing maritime traffic; the dual US-Iran blockade of the Gulf remained in force.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon (Hezbollah)
theatres.status: US-brokered ceasefire announced but Hezbollah rejects it; Israeli strikes and evacuation orders continue

timeline.event: Israeli operations in Lebanon intensified, prompting Iran to cite them as a reason for suspending talks with Washington; Israel said its campaign against Hezbollah infrastructure would continue.

The US, Israel and Lebanon issued a joint statement announcing a signed ceasefire agreement, reportedly conditioned on Hezbollah's withdrawal south of the Litani River, a halt to its fire, and Lebanese Armed Forces-only 'pilot zones'.

Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected the ceasefire negotiated without the group, demanding a comprehensive cessation of hostilities and full Israeli withdrawal; Israel and Hezbollah traded fresh strikes hours after the truce was announced, and Israeli strikes killed at least four in Lebanon per local reports.

Israel continued strikes across Lebanon despite the announced ceasefire, including in the Naqoura, Nabatieh and Tyre areas (at least one death reported), and the IDF ordered residents of at least nine southern Lebanese towns to evacuate. Defence Minister Israel Katz said operations in south Lebanon would continue.

theatres.name: Israel–Gaza
theatres.status: October 2025 ceasefire nominal; Israeli strikes continue killing roughly 9–11 Palestinians per day

timeline.event: Israel and Hamas began a ceasefire in Gaza, though both sides and rights groups have since reported repeated violations.

The UN's Deputy Special Coordinator urged the Security Council to consolidate the Gaza ceasefire and halt escalating violence and settlement activity in the West Bank, underscoring the truce's fragility.

Israeli strikes killed at least 10 Palestinians, hospitals said; nine were killed in at least four overnight strikes in Gaza City, including two women and two children, per Shifa Hospital, and another evening strike killed at least one person.

Reporting on continued Israeli strikes put the prior 24-hour toll at about 11 Palestinians killed and dozens wounded across Gaza, with analysts noting the ceasefire lacks enforcement. Cumulative deaths since the October 2025 truce are local/contested estimates.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: Four outbreaks are active. The Bundibugyo-virus Ebola epidemic in DR Congo and Uganda — a WHO-declared PHEIC since 17 May 2026 — keeps escalating; the WHO Director-General's 3 June briefing cited roughly 359 confirmed cases and 61 deaths (DRC 344/60, Uganda 15/1), with one exported case treated in Germany and DRC contact-tracing still weak. US measles has reached 2,030 confirmed cases (0 deaths) across 40 jurisdictions plus DC — the highest annual total since 1992, putting elimination status at risk. A multi-country cholera/AWD outbreak remains 'very high' risk (~79,740 cases, ~1,010 deaths across 22 countries in 2026 to late April). The MV Hondius Andes hantavirus cluster (13 cases, 3 deaths) appears contained, with no new cases in over two weeks.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.scope_note: DRC+Uganda combined CONFIRMED cases/deaths per WHO Director-General media briefing 3 Jun 2026: DRC 344/60, Uganda 15/1; total ~359 confirmed / 61 deaths. Suspected cases cut to 116 from >1,000 the prior week. Agency tallies vary: WHO DON605 (publ. 29 May) listed 134 confirmed/18 deaths plus 906 suspected in DRC — the 3 Jun briefing supersedes it. One exported confirmed case (US citizen) under care in Germany; WHO sometimes counts 3 countries. CFR ~25–50%; no approved vaccine/therapeutic. Primary: WHO.

outbreaks.name: Measles — United States (2026)
outbreaks.scope_note: Confirmed US cases in calendar 2026 per US CDC measles data (as of 4 Jun 2026): 2,030 cases, 0 deaths, across 40 jurisdictions plus DC (and NYC); 30 outbreaks; ~92% in unvaccinated or unknown-status persons. Highest US annual total since 1992; US projected to lose measles elimination status later in 2026. Primary: US CDC (corroborated via CIDRAP).

outbreaks.name: Cholera — multi-country outbreak
outbreaks.scope_note: Cholera and acute watery diarrhoea (AWD) cases/deaths worldwide 1 Jan–26 Apr 2026 per WHO multi-country cholera epidemiological update #37 (publ. 29 May 2026): 79,740 cases, 1,010 deaths, 22 countries across 4 WHO regions. April 2026 alone: ~20,100 new cases (+20% MoM), 239 deaths; top countries DRC (4,710), Angola (1,760), South Sudan (1,528). Cumulative figure includes suspected cases. Primary: WHO.

outbreaks.name: Andes hantavirus — MV Hondius cruise ship cluster
outbreaks.status: Likely contained; no new cases >2 weeks (count stale)
outbreaks.scope_note: Cases/deaths per WHO DON604 (as of 27 May 2026): 13 cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable), 3 deaths, CFR ~23%. Linked to Netherlands-flagged MV Hondius; contacts traced across ~32 countries/territories, cases hospitalised/quarantined in ~12 countries incl. Singapore. CDC situation page (updated 2 Jun 2026) reports monitored individuals symptom-free and global risk 'extremely low' but gives no new case count — case count now >2 weeks old (flagged stale). Primary: WHO / US CDC / ECDC.

stories.headline: WHO chief: Bundibugyo Ebola cases climb in DRC and Uganda as contact tracing lags
stories.summary: At a 3 June media briefing, the WHO Director-General reported 344 confirmed cases and 60 deaths in DRC and 15 cases/1 death in Uganda, with suspected cases cut to 116 from over 1,000 a week earlier. Only ~45% of DRC contacts are being followed up; insecurity and population movement hamper the response. One infected US citizen is being treated in Germany.

stories.headline: WHO declares Bundibugyo Ebola epidemic a Public Health Emergency of International Concern
stories.summary: WHO determined that the Ebola disease outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus in DR Congo and Uganda constitutes a PHEIC, citing rapid cross-border spread, a virus with no approved vaccine or therapeutic, and an estimated case fatality rate of 25–50%.

stories.headline: US measles outbreak tops 2,000 known infections
stories.summary: US measles cases reached 2,030 confirmed for 2026 (0 deaths) across 40 jurisdictions plus DC per CDC, the highest annual total since 1992. About 92% of cases are in unvaccinated or unknown-status people; analysts warn the US is highly likely to lose its measles elimination status later in 2026.

stories.headline: WHO cholera update #37: 79,740 cases and 1,010 deaths in 2026, global risk 'very high'
stories.summary: WHO's multi-country cholera epidemiological update #37 (29 May) reported 79,740 cholera/AWD cases and 1,010 deaths from 22 countries between 1 January and 26 April 2026. April saw a 20% rise in new cases, led by DRC (4,710), Angola (1,760) and South Sudan (1,528).

stories.headline: CDC: Andes hantavirus cruise-ship cluster contained; monitored travellers remain symptom-free
stories.summary: CDC's situation summary (updated 2 June) reports monitored individuals from the MV Hondius Andes hantavirus cluster remain symptom-free, with no new cases. The outbreak stands at 13 cases and 3 deaths (WHO, 27 May); CDC assesses pandemic and US public risk as extremely low.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: This cycle is dominated by actively-exploited, internet-facing vulnerabilities: an unpatched Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager zero-day (CVE-2026-20245) granting root, a critical Cisco Unified CM SSRF (CVE-2026-20230) with public PoC, and three WordPress/Magento flaws (Everest Forms CVE-2026-3300, WP Maps Pro CVE-2026-8732, Magento/Mirasvit CVE-2026-45247) seeing mass exploitation and CISA KEV additions. AI-tooling supply-chain and prompt-injection risk is a strong secondary theme — a Claude Code GitHub Action flaw, the Miasma/IronWorm npm worm hitting Red Hat packages, a trojanized OpenAI Codex npm package, and Gemini notification hijacks. Several serious flaws ship with no vendor fix (Windows Search URI NTLM leak, HTTP/2 Bomb DoS), so defenders must act on mitigations rather than wait for patches. Notable breaches and takedowns round out the week: DentaQuest (2.6M), UN WFP Gaza (600K households), and a Dutch takedown of a 17M-device proxy botnet.

stories.headline: Hackers exploit critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress plugin RCE (CVE-2026-3300)
stories.summary: Attackers are actively exploiting an eval()-based RCE in Everest Forms Pro (~4,000 installs) to fully compromise sites; over 29,300 exploit attempts blocked, payloads creating an admin account named 'diksimarina'. Patched in v1.9.13 (March 18, 2026); all versions through 1.9.12 affected.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN Manager zero-day under active exploitation, no patch (CVE-2026-20245)
stories.summary: Cisco warned of an unpatched, actively exploited flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (insufficient input validation) that lets a low-privileged netadmin upload a crafted file and run commands as root; Mandiant observed exploitation pushing config changes to edge devices. No fix yet — Cisco points to chaining with CVE-2026-20182/20127.

stories.headline: Over 900 US gas-station tank gauge systems exposed to attack
stories.summary: Shadowserver found 909 US Automatic Tank Gauge systems internet-exposed on port 10001/tcp, with flaws including hardcoded credentials, auth bypass, SQL injection and OS command execution. Attackers could alter settings, disable alerts and risk leaks or permanent tank damage; a May report tied Iranian actors to manipulating ATG readings.

stories.headline: New threat cluster OP-512 targets Microsoft IIS servers with custom web shell framework
stories.summary: ReliaQuest disclosed a previously unreported, China-linked espionage cluster (OP-512) deploying a bespoke three-web-shell framework on Microsoft IIS servers, using timestomping and anti-forensics to evade detection. It is the fourth IIS-focused actor seen in 12 months and was observed hitting a legacy Windows Server 2016 / .NET 4.0 host.

stories.headline: Cisco patches critical Unified CM SSRF with public PoC (CVE-2026-20230)
stories.summary: An unauthenticated SSRF/file-write flaw in the Cisco Unified CM WebDialer service lets a remote attacker write files and escalate to root; Cisco rates it Critical despite the 8.6 base score. Public PoC exploit code is available; WebDialer is disabled by default.

stories.headline: Claude Code GitHub Action flaw let one malicious issue hijack repositories
stories.summary: GMO Flatt Security's RyotaK showed an indirect prompt-injection in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action: a single crafted issue could trick Claude into leaking OIDC/installation credentials from /proc/self/environ, yielding write access to a repo's code, issues and workflows — and potentially the action itself. Anthropic rated it 7.8 (CVSS v4.0) and fixed it within four days (claude-code-action v1.0.94).

stories.headline: DentaQuest data breach exposed 2.6 million accounts
stories.summary: Extortion group ShinyHunters stole 234GB from dental-benefits administrator DentaQuest (part of Sun Life) and, after failed negotiations, leaked data on 2.6M accounts — names, emails, phone numbers, government IDs, health-insurance info and dates of birth. DentaQuest confirmed 'unauthorized access to a limited portion' of its network on June 2.

stories.headline: UN World Food Programme breach affects 600,000 Gaza households
stories.summary: A May 14 breach of the WFP's Palestine self-registration application exposed names, ID numbers, phone numbers and neighborhood/location data for roughly 600,000 Gaza households. WFP disclosed via Telegram on June 1, suspended the platform, and warned beneficiaries about impersonation scams while assistance continues.

stories.headline: Hola Browser for Windows compromised to deliver Monero cryptominer
stories.summary: A supply-chain compromise of the Windows Hola Browser shipped an undeclared 'me.exe' Monero miner that added Defender exclusions, installed itself as a 'hola_monitor_svc' service and mined while idle. Hola says ~0.1% of users were affected with no evidence of data theft, and is rebuilding its distribution pipeline.

stories.headline: WhatsApp/Slack notifications could hijack Google Gemini on Android
stories.summary: SafeBreach detailed indirect prompt-injection against Gemini's Android notification-reading 'Utilities' feature: any app that can push a notification (WhatsApp, Slack, Signal, SMS, etc.) can deliver instructions Gemini acts on, using a 'Fake Context Alignment' foreign-language authorization trick. Google says content-classifier fixes (confirmed Nov 14, 2025) mitigate it; no in-the-wild use.

stories.headline: CISA adds exploited Magento (Mirasvit Cache Warmer) RCE to KEV (CVE-2026-45247)
stories.summary: CISA added a critical PHP object-deserialization RCE in the Mirasvit Cache Warmer Magento/Adobe Commerce extension to KEV after Imperva observed in-the-wild exploitation via base64 serialized payloads in the CacheWarmer cookie. Affects all versions before 1.11.12; patched May 25; FCEB deadline June 6.

stories.headline: Autonomous AI tool finds 2-year-old Redis RCE (CVE-2026-23479)
stories.summary: Theori's autonomous 'Xint Code' tool found a use-after-free in Redis blocking-client code (introduced in 7.2.0, present 2+ years) that lets an authenticated user run OS commands on the host. NVD rates it 8.8 (CVSS 3.1); Redis lists 7.7 (CVSS 4.0). Patched May 5; many cloud Redis instances run without a password.

stories.headline: Microsoft 365 Android apps leaked account tokens via leftover debug flag (FlagLeft)
stories.summary: Enclave's 'FlagLeft' research found a setIsDebugMode(true) flag left on in production builds of Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Loop and OneNote, disabling the trusted-app check so any installed app could grab the signed-in user's reusable 'FOCI' token and read mail/files/calendar without prompts. Microsoft issued four CVEs on May 12 (CVE-2026-41100 4.4, -41101 7.1, -41102 7.1, -42832 7.7); details public June 2.

stories.headline: 'HTTP/2 Bomb' enables remote DoS on NGINX, Apache, IIS, Envoy & Cloudflare (CVE-2026-49975)
stories.summary: A new HPACK-amplification + Slowloris DoS lets a single client exhaust ~32GB of server memory in ~20 seconds against default HTTP/2 configs, potentially threatening 880,000+ sites. nginx fixed it in 1.29.8 (max_headers default 1,000) and Apache in mod_http2 v2.0.41; at disclosure no fixes were available for IIS, Envoy or Cloudflare Pingora.

stories.headline: Google June 2026 Android update patches 124 flaws, one exploited zero-day (CVE-2025-48595)
stories.summary: Android's June 2026 update fixes 124 flaws including an actively exploited Framework integer-overflow privilege-escalation zero-day (CVE-2025-48595) needing no user interaction, affecting Android 14–16 QPR2. CISA added it to KEV on June 2 (FCEB deadline June 5); the limited, targeted exploitation pattern suggests spyware/nation-state use.

stories.headline: Chinese hackers deploy new Atlas RAT in European cyberattacks
stories.summary: A China-linked group is using a newly identified 'Atlas RAT' in cyberattacks against European targets, expanding the toolset seen in recent China-aligned espionage activity across the region.

stories.headline: Unpatched Windows Search URI flaw leaks NTLMv2 hashes (no CVE, won't fix)
stories.summary: Huntress disclosed an NTLMv2-hash leak in the Windows 'search:' URI handler: a crafted link with a 'crumb=location:' parameter forces an SMB connection to an attacker server, exposing hashes for relay. The primitive is identical to the Snipping Tool's CVE-2026-33829, but after April 15 disclosure Microsoft declined to fix it, citing its servicing bar. Mitigate by blocking outbound SMB, enforcing SMB signing and disabling NTLM.

stories.headline: US sanctions Iran's Nobitex crypto exchange tied to ransomware actors
stories.summary: OFAC sanctioned Nobitex — Iran's largest crypto exchange, which processed over 50% of Iranian digital-asset inflows in 2025 — plus Wallex, Bitpin, Ramzinex and Nobitex executives, citing IRGC-linked transactions and wallets tied to IRGC-affiliated ransomware actors.

stories.headline: Oracle WebLogic CVE-2024-21182 added to CISA KEV after active exploitation
stories.summary: CISA added a two-year-old Oracle WebLogic flaw (unauthenticated takeover via T3/IIOP, patched July 2024) to KEV on June 1 after observing exploitation; FCEB deadline June 4. Shodan shows 1,592+ exposed, vulnerable WebLogic servers still online.

stories.headline: Dashlane discloses brute-force attack; encrypted vaults of fewer than 20 users downloaded
stories.summary: Dashlane disclosed a May 31 campaign that brute-forced short-lived 2FA codes to register attacker devices on targeted accounts, enabling download of encrypted vaults for fewer than 20 users before automated detection locked them. Vault contents remain protected by the user's Master Password; no internal-system compromise.

stories.headline: Critical WP Maps Pro flaw actively exploited to create rogue admin accounts (CVE-2026-8732)
stories.summary: An unauthenticated 'temporary access' AJAX endpoint in WP Maps Pro (relying only on a client-side nonce) lets attackers create admin accounts on 15,000+ sites. Wordfence blocked ~2,858 attacks in 24 hours; 3,600+ attempts observed. Affects versions <=6.1.0; fixed in 6.1.1 (May 20).

stories.headline: Miasma/IronWorm npm supply-chain attack compromises Red Hat packages
stories.summary: A self-propagating npm worm (Miasma, a Shai-Hulud variant; tracked by BleepingComputer as IronWorm) compromised ~32 @redhat-cloud-services packages on June 1 via a hijacked Red Hat employee's GitHub account. Malicious preinstall scripts steal developer credentials and AWS/GCP/Azure cloud identities at install time and republish to other accessible packages (~80K weekly downloads combined). Red Hat says no official products shipped the compromised versions.

stories.headline: OpenAI Codex auth tokens stolen via malicious codexui-android npm package
stories.summary: A legitimate-looking 'codexui-android' npm package (29,000+ weekly downloads) was updated to silently exfiltrate OpenAI Codex credentials from the plaintext auth.json to a server mimicking Sentry; a companion Android app runs it in a PRoot sandbox. Because OpenAI refresh tokens don't expire, attackers can impersonate victims indefinitely.

stories.headline: Dutch authorities dismantle 17-million-device proxy botnet (Asocks)
stories.summary: On May 28 Dutch police and the NCSC seized servers (200+ NL-based) backing a botnet of at least 17 million infected computers, phones and IoT devices tied to Asocks, a Russia-based residential/mobile proxy service that resold victims' bandwidth without their knowledge.

stories.headline: PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass under active exploitation (CVE-2026-0257)
stories.summary: Attackers are forging authentication-override cookies against PAN-OS GlobalProtect portals/gateways that reuse a certificate, achieving auth bypass and unauthorized VPN access. Rapid7 first saw exploitation May 17; CISA added it to KEV with a June 1 mitigation deadline. Mitigate by disabling auth-override or using a dedicated certificate.
